calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-10-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donald Mah for Dorothy Tebo.

Todd Christansen, agent for the bond company waived notice. Mr. Mah stated that the assets would be given back to the Petitioner. The Court granted the petition, (signing order for the same in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison CSR#4609. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-20-2008 Continued To Jun-10-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-15-2008 Off Calendar

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-18-2008 Continued To Apr-15-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donald Mah for Dorothy Tebo.

Mr. Mah informed the Court that the inventory and apprasial had been signed and that he would be sending the petition for final distribution for authorization. The matter was continued to April 15, 2008. If the petition for final distribution is not filed by that date, the Court will impose a surcharge of 25.00 per day.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Kathy Kollehner CSR#4102. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Santiago.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Feb-27-2008 Continued To Mar-18-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Don Mah for Dorothy Tebo.

Mr. Mah stated that there was not much in this estate, and that he needed to do a final distribution. The matter was continued to March 18, 2008 for status on filing the petition for final distribution. No appearance is necessary on that date if the petition is filed.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Leticia Perez CSR#7782. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-09-2008 Continued To Feb-27-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Don Mah for Dorothy Tebo (Administrator).

Mr. Mah reported that he was still struggling with Wells Fargo, that he still does not have the information, and suggested the Court issue a citition to Wells Fargo. Mr. Mah will draft the citation. The matter was continued to February 27, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Lee.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-18-2007 Continued To Jan-09-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES- ATTY. TOM JOHNSON SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR ATTY. DONALD MAH;

ATTY. JOHNSON IS REQUESTING A CONTINUANCE ON THIS MATTER, MR. MAH IS PRESENTLY ILL. THE COURT RAISED SOME CONCERNS REGARDING ISSUES NOT BEING COMPLIED BY MR. MAH. THE COURT CONTINUED THE MATTER TO JANUARY 9, 2008 AND A SANCTION OF $25.00 PER DAY WILL BE ISSUED TO MR. MAH UNTIL HE COMPLIES WITH THE CONTROVERTED ISSUES.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR #11722 Clerk: Cynthia Herbert. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Oct-31-2007 Continued To Dec-18-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donald Mah for Dorothy Tebo (Administtator).

Mr. Mah stated that Wells Fargo Bank was not happy with the subpeona and requested a new one be signed by the Court. The Court granted his request signing the Subpeona for Records and continued the matter to November 14, 2007.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Doug Handa.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Aug-22-2007 Continued To Oct-31-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Donald Mah appearing for administrator Dorothy Tebo. Counsel reported that Michael Tang does not have the funds. He is looking for information from Wells Fargo. The estate has no assets unless there are money in the account. Court noted this matter has been continued many times. Matter is continued to 10/31. Counsel can file a status report sooner if he gets the information.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Lee.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jul-03-2007 Continued To Aug-22-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Donald Mah appearing for Dorothy Tebo. Counsel's old firm has expended. His client has not sign a substitution of attorney. Counsel has found no probate assets. Counsel is requesting for continuance. Matter is continued to 8/22. No further continuance is allowed.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar May-09-2007 Continued To Jul-03-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Donald Mah appearing for administrator Dorothy Tebo. He is still working on the report. The administrator has informed him that she is still waiting for a death certificate. She has not found any money only debts. Matter is continued to 7/3.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Apr-24-2007 Continued To May-09-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Donald Mah appearing for petitioner. He is still waiting for the report. Matter is continued to 5/9.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Soret-Matley CSR# 9225. Clerk: Ella Yip.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel
Off Calendar Mar-14-2007 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donald Mah (counsel for Dorothy Tebo, Personal Representative); Jonathan Ferdon (Counsel for the San Francisco Public Administrator); Todd Christensen (for Phillips Bonding Agency).

Mr. Mah advised the Court that his firm had achieved some degree of rapproachemnt with its client and would be withdrawing its Motion to Withdraw as Counsel. After further discussion concerning the status of the case, what assets may or may not be subject to probate, and issues concerning the outstanding bond, the parties agreed to retroactively set the bond at $20,000.00 from the date of issuance of the original bond with Phillips Bonding advancing the premium payment on the reduced bond to the Estate for later reimbursement. The Court continued the hearing on pending matters to April 24, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 and requested that Mr. Ferdon appear at that time.

udicial Officer: John Dearman, Judge; Court Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: Deputy Teresa Fox; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Christine Nahnsen.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Mar-14-2007 Continued To Apr-24-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Feb-14-2007 Continued To Mar-14-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Donald Mah (Counsel for Dorothy Tebo, Personal Representative); Jonathan Ferdon (Counsel for the San Francisco Public Administrator); Todd Christensen (for Phillips Bonding Agency).

Mr. Mah noted at the outset that his firm was moving to withdraw from representation of the personal representative and as such was not in a position to prepare or file the required Inventory and Appraisal, much to the surprise and consternation of Mr. Ferdon. Given the procedural posture of the case, Mr. Ferdon represented that the Public Administrator would decline to serve as personal representative at this time. In addition, the Court declined to discharge the bond of Phillips Bonding given the circumstances of the case. In light of the status of the case, the Court continued the hearing on all pending matters to March 14, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 to be heard at the same time as Mr. Mah's Motion to Withdraw.

Judicial Officer: Stella Pantazis, Judge Pro Tempore; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Bailiff: Deputy Jimmy Peralta; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Christine Nahnson.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-31-2007 Continued To Feb-14-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Todd Christensen appearing for Philips Bonding. Mr. Mah is not available this morning. He wanted a continuance to 2/14. Mr. Ferdon has received some affidavits from the family. Court wants to see them. Court will let Mr. Ferdon know what he should do with the documents. Matter is continued to 2/14.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Jan-17-2007 Continued To Jan-31-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Donald Mah appearing for administrator Dorothy Tebo. Todd Christensen appearing for Phillips Bonding. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for Public Administrator. Ms. Tebo has unable to pay the bond premium. Mr. Mah wants to have the bond exonerated from the date it was issued. The administrator has not taken control of the estate assets. Mr. Christensen asked to exonerate the bond or have the beneficiary paid the bond premium. Mr. Mah said the beneficiary does not have the money to pay and they want the Public Administrator to take over. Mr. Ferdon said they will need more information before they can take over this case. Court wants Mr. Mah to look for the bank account. Matter is continued to 1/31.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jun-21-2006 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Donald Mah appearing for petitioner. Jonathan Ferdon appearing for P.A.. The P.A. is willing to give the family an opportunity to work things out. Court wants counsel to discuss the issue outside of court.
At 10:10am, both counsel return to court. Mr. Ferdon agrees to waive priority and to have the grandmother appointed with bond. Mr. Mah agrees to give Mr. Ferdon a status report. Bond is fixed in the amount of $250,000.00. Matter granted with full IAEA. Mr. Mah will prepare an order. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-22-2006 Continued To Jun-12-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Donald Mah appearing for petitioner. Petitioner does not have priority. The PA has priority. Court wants counsel to notice the Public Administrator. No need to re-publish. Matter is continued to 6/12. Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar May-01-2006 Continued To May-22-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
